mainEntity.name: How am I supposed to afford a lawyer to fight this denial if I'm not even getting paid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most workers' comp attorneys work on contingency, meaning there are no upfront costs. They only get paid a percentage of the benefits they recover for you. You don't need money out-of-pocket to hire elite representation.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Many workers' comp lawyers, including us, work on a 'contingency fee' basis. This means we only get paid if we win your case or secure a settlement. In many states like Utah, the attorney fees are set by the Labor Commission and are taken as a percentage of the 'back-dated' benefits we recover for you. You should never have to pay a retainer upfront to fight a medical denial.

Factors: The complexity of the medical evidence and whether the case goes to a full evidentiary hearing.

Look for a firm that offers a 'free consultation.' This allows you to get your denial letter reviewed without spending a dime.

Because the insurance company has unlimited money to fight you, having a contingency-fee lawyer levels the playing field without any financial risk to you.
